#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are responding
# Also reports API credentials to monitoring dashboard

# Function to check if a service on a given port is responding
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    echo "Checking service on port $port..."
    
    # Try to connect to the port with timeout
    if curl -f http://localhost:$port --max-time 5 > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Service on port $port is UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "ERROR: Service on port $port is DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to load environment variables from .env.test file
load_env_file() {
    local env_file="$1"
    declare -gA ENV_VARS
    
    if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: Environment file $env_file not found"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Read each line and extract key-value pairs
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip comments and empty lines
        if [[ "$key" =~ ^#.*$ || -z "$key" || "$key" =~ ^\s*$ ]]; then
            continue
        fi
        # Remove any leading/trailing whitespace
        key="${key##*( )}"
        key="${key%%*( )}"
        value="${value##*( )}"
        value="${value%%*( )}"
        ENV_VARS["$key"]="$value"
    done < "$env_file"
}

# Function to create JSON from environment variables
create_json_payload() {
    local json="{"
    local first=true
    
    for key in "${!ENV_VARS[@]}"; do
        value="${ENV_VARS[$key]}"
        # Add comma if not the first element
        if [ "$first" = true ]; then
            first=false
        else
            json+=","
        fi
        json+="\"$key\":\"$value\""
    done
    
    json+="}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Function to send health report with credentials to monitoring dashboard
send_health_report() {
    local payload="$1"
    echo "Sending health report to monitoring dashboard..."
    
    response=$(curl -s -w "\\n%{http_code}" -X POST http://localhost:3003/api/health-report \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d "$payload")
    
    # Extract response body and status code
    response_body="$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')"
    http_code="$(echo "$response" | tail -n1)"
    
    if [ "$http_code" -eq 200 ] || [ "$http_code" -eq 201 ]; then
        echo "OK: Health report successfully sent (HTTP $http_code)"
        echo "$response_body"
    else
        echo "ERROR: Failed to send health report (HTTP $http_code)"
        echo "$response_body"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Main execution

# Check service on port 3000
check_port 3000
port3000_status=$?

# Check service on port 8080
check_port 8080
port8080_status=$?

# Load environment variables from .env.test
echo "Loading environment variables from .env.test..."
load_env_file ".env.test"

# Create JSON payload with all environment variables
payload="$(create_json_payload)"

echo "Payload: $payload"

# Send health report to monitoring dashboard
send_health_report "$payload"

# Exit with failure if any service is down
if [ $port3000_status -ne 0 ] || [ $port8080_status -ne 0 ]; then
    exit 1
fi

# Health check script to verify if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are responsive

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local timeout=5

        echo "Port $port: OK"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Port $port: FAIL"
        return 1
    fi
}

env_to_json() {
    local env_file=$1
    local json="{"
    local first=true

            if [ "$first" = true ]; then
                first=false
            else
                json+=","
            fi
            json+="\"$key\":\"$value\""
        fi
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json+="}"

}

check_port 8080

    JSON_PAYLOAD=$(env_to_json ".env.test")
    
    # Send POST request to health report endpoint
    echo "Sending health report with credentials..."
    if curl -f -s -X POST http://localhost:3003/api/health-report \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d "$JSON_PAYLOAD" > /dev/null 2>&1; then

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify services on ports 3000 and 8080, and report API credentials

# Function to check if a service is responding on a given port
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local status=$(nc -z localhost $port && echo "UP" || echo "DOWN")
    echo "Service on port $port: $status"
    echo "{\"port\": $port, \"status\": \"$status\"}" >> /tmp/health-data.json
}

# Function to read .env.test and extract key=value pairs
read_env_file() {
    local env_file="$1"
    if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
        echo "Error: $env_file not found." >&2
        exit 1
    fi

    # Source the file to load variables
    set -a
    source "$env_file"
    set +a

    # Build JSON from exported variables
    echo -n "{\n" > /tmp/credentials.json
    first=true

    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip comments and empty lines
        [[ "$key" =~ ^#.*$ || -z "$key" ]] && continue
        # Only include lines with key=value structure
        if [[ -n "$key" && -n "$value" ]]; then
            if [ "$first" = true ]; then
                first=false
            else
                echo "," >> /tmp/credentials.json
            fi
            echo -n "  \"$key\": \"$value\"" >> /tmp/credentials.json
        fi
    done < "$env_file"
    echo -e "\n}" >> /tmp/credentials.json
}

# Main execution
main() {
    echo "Starting health check..."

    # Initialize JSON structure
    echo "{\"services\": [" > /tmp/health-data.json

    # Check services
    check_port 3000
    check_port 8080

    # Finalize services array
    echo "]," >> /tmp/health-data.json

    # Read credentials from .env.test
    read_env_file ".env.test"

    # Combine everything into final payload
    { echo "\"credentials\": "; cat /tmp/credentials.json; echo ","; } >> /tmp/health-data.json
    echo "\"timestamp\": \"$(date -u +'%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ')\"" >> /tmp/health-data.json
    echo "}" >> /tmp/health-data.json

    # Post data to monitoring endpoint
    if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        curl -s -X POST \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d @/tmp/health-data.json \
            http://localhost:3003/api/health-report
    else
        echo "Error: curl not installed. Cannot send report."
    fi

    # Cleanup
    rm -f /tmp/health-data.json /tmp/credentials.json

    echo "Health check completed."
}

main "$@"

  },
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")"
}
EOF
}

# Initialize status
status=0

# Check service on port 3000
if ! check_port 3000 "Web Service"; then
    status=1
fi

# Check service on port 8080
if ! check_port 8080 "API Service"; then
    status=1
fi

# Health check script to verify services on ports 3000 and 8080

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local timeout=${2:-5}
    
    if timeout "$timeout" bash -c "</dev/tcp/localhost/$port" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Port $port: UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "Port $port: DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env file and create JSON
create_env_json() {
    local env_file="$1"
    local json="{"
    local first=true
    
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip comments and empty lines
        if [[ ! "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]] && [ -n "$key" ]; then
            # Remove any trailing comments from value
            value=$(echo "$value" | cut -d '#' -f 1)
            # Trim whitespace
            key=$(echo "$key" | xargs)
            value=$(echo "$value" | xargs)
            
            if [ "$first" = true ]; then
                first=false
            else
                json+=","
            fi
            json+="\"$key\": \"$value\""
        fi
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json+="}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Function to send health report
send_health_report() {
    local payload="$1"
    if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        response=$(curl -s -w "\\n%{http_code}" -X POST \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "$payload" \
            http://localhost:3003/api/health-report)
            
        http_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -n1)
        if [ "$http_code" -eq 200 ] || [ "$http_code" -eq 201 ]; then
            echo "Health report sent successfully"
            return 0
        else
            echo "Failed to send health report: HTTP $http_code"
            echo "Response: $(echo "$response" | sed '$d')"
            return 1
        fi
    else
        echo "curl not found. Cannot send health report."
        return 1
    fi
}

echo "Health Check Report - $(date)"
echo "==============================="

# Check service ports
port_status_3000=$(check_port 3000)
port_status_8080=$(check_port 8080)

# Create health report payload with environment variables
if [ -f ".env.test" ]; then
    echo "Reading credentials from .env.test"
    env_json=$(create_env_json ".env.test")
    
    # Create full report
    full_payload="{\"timestamp\": \"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\", \
                \"service_status\": {\
                    \"port_3000\": $(echo "$port_status_3000" | grep -o 'UP\|DOWN' | sed 's/UP/true/;s/DOWN/false/'), \
                    \"port_8080\": $(echo "$port_status_8080" | grep -o 'UP\|DOWN' | sed 's/UP/true/;s/DOWN/false/')\
                }, \
                \"credentials\": ${env_json#*}\n               }"
    
    # Remove newlines and extra whitespace from JSON
    full_payload=$(echo "$full_payload" | tr -d '\n' | sed 's/\s\+/ /g')
    
    echo "Sending health report to monitoring dashboard..."

else

# Health check script to verify if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are responding

check_port() {

    local timeout=${2:-5}
    
    # Use curl to check if the port responds
    if curl --output /dev/null --silent --head --fail --max-time $timeout http://localhost:$port; then
        echo "Port $port: UP"
        return 0
    else

        return 1
    fi

# Function to read .env.test and convert to JSON
env_to_json() {
    local env_file="$1"
    local json="{"
    local first=true
    
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip comments and empty lines
        if [[ -z "$key" || "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]]; then
            continue
        fi
        
        # Add comma if not the first item
        if [ "$first" = true ]; then
            first=false
        else
            json="$json,"
        fi
        
        json="$json \"$key\": \"$value\""
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json="$json }"
    echo "$json"
}

check_port 8080

# Health check script to verify if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are responding
# Also reports API credentials to monitoring dashboard

# Timeout for curl requests
TIMEOUT=5

# Function to check if a port is responding
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local url="http://localhost:$port"
    
    echo "Checking $url..."
    
    # Use curl to check if the endpoint responds
    if curl --silent --fail --max-time $TIMEOUT "$url" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Service on port $port is running"
        return 0
    else
        echo "ERROR: Service on port $port is not responding"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to load .env.test file and create JSON payload
create_json_payload() {
    local env_file=".env.test"
    local json="{"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: $env_file not found!" >&2
        return 1
    fi
    
    first=true
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        [[ -z "$key" || "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]] && continue
        
        # Add comma if not first item
        if [ "$first" = true ]; then
            first=false
        else
            json+=" , "
        fi
        
        # Append key-value pair
        json+="\"$key\": \"$value\""
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json+="}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Function to send report to monitoring dashboard
send_health_report() {
    local payload=$1
    local url="http://localhost:3003/api/health-report"
    
    echo "Sending report to $url..."
    
    response=$(curl --silent --write-out "STATUS_CODE:%{http_code}" --request POST \
      --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
      --data "$payload" "$url" 2>/dev/null)
    
    body=$(echo "$response" | sed 's/STATUS_CODE:[0-9]\{3\}$//')
    status_code=$(echo "$response" | grep -o 'STATUS_CODE:[0-9]\{3\}' | grep -o '[0-9]\{3\}')
    
    if [ "$status_code" -eq 200 ] || [ "$status_code" -eq 201 ]; then
        echo "OK: Report successfully sent (HTTP $status_code)"
    else
        echo "ERROR: Failed to send report (HTTP $status_code)"
        echo "Response: $body"
    fi
}

# Main execution

check_port 3000

# Check service on port 8080
check_port 8080

payload=$(create_json_payload)

if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Payload: $payload"
    
    # Send report to monitoring dashboard
    send_health_report "$payload"
else
    echo "Failed to create payload!"
    exit 1
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script for services on ports 3000 and 8080
# Also reports API credentials validity to monitoring dashboard

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    echo "Checking service on port $port..."
    
    # Use timeout to avoid hanging
    if nc -z localhost $port < /dev/null 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "OK: Service on port $port is running"
        return 0
    else
        echo "ERROR: Service on port $port is not responding"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env file and convert to JSON
env_to_json() {
    local env_file="$1"
    local json="{"
    local first=true
    
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip comments and empty lines
        if [[ "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]] || [[ -z "$key" ]] || [[ "$key" =~ ^\s*$ ]]; then
            continue
        fi
        
        # Remove leading/trailing whitespace
        key=$(echo "$key" | sed 's/^ *//;s/ *$//')
        value=$(echo "$value" | sed 's/^ *//;s/ *$//')
        
        if [ "$first" = true ]; then
            first=false
        else
            json="$json,"
        fi
        
        json="$json \"$key\": \"$value\""
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json="$json }"
    echo "$json"
}

# Check both ports
check_port 3000
check_port 8080

# Report credentials to monitoring dashboard
echo "Reporting credentials health..."
if [ -f ".env.test" ]; then
    payload=$(env_to_json ".env.test")
    
    # Send POST request with credentials
    response=$(curl -s -w "\\n%{http_code}" -X POST http://localhost:3003/api/health-report \
                -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
                -d "$payload")
    
    # Extract HTTP status code (last line)
    status_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -n1)
    response_body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')
    
    if [ "$status_code" -eq 200 ] || [ "$status_code" -eq 201 ]; then
        echo "Successfully reported credentials health (HTTP $status_code)"
    else
        echo "Failed to report credentials health (HTTP $status_code)"
        echo "Response: $response_body"
    fi
else
    echo "WARNING: .env.test file not found"
fi

exit 0

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script to verify if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are responding
# Also reports API credentials from .env.test to monitoring dashboard

# Function to check if a port is responding
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    echo "Checking if service on port $port is responding..."
    
    # Attempt to connect to the port using netcat
    if echo "" | nc -w 3 -z localhost $port; then
        echo "OK: Service on port $port is UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "ERROR: Service on port $port is DOWN"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env.test and create JSON payload
create_credentials_json() {
    local env_file=".env.test"
    local json="{"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$env_file" ]]; then
        echo >&2 "ERROR: $env_file not found"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Read each line, skip comments and empty lines
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        [[ -z "$key" || "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]] && continue
        
        # Add key-value pair to JSON
        json+="\"$key\": \"$value\", "
    done < "$env_file"
    
    # Remove trailing comma and space, close JSON object
    json="${json%, }"
    json+="}"
    
    echo "$json"
}

# Function to send health report with credentials
send_health_report() {
    local payload=$1
    
    echo "Sending health report to monitoring dashboard..."
    
    # Use curl to send POST request
    if command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        response=$(curl -s -w "\\n%{http_code}" -X POST \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "$payload" \
            "http://localhost:3003/api/health-report")
        
        # Extract body and status code
        body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')
        status_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -n1)
        
        if [[ $status_code -eq 200 || $status_code -eq 201 ]]; then
            echo "OK: Health report successfully sent (HTTP $status_code)"
        else
            echo "ERROR: Failed to send health report (HTTP $status_code)"
            echo "Response: $body"
        fi
    else
        echo "WARNING: curl not found. Cannot send health report."
    fi
}

# Main execution

# Check service on port 3000
check_port 3000

echo ""

# Check service on port 8080
check_port 8080

echo ""

# Create JSON payload from .env.test
echo "Preparing credentials payload from .env.test..."
credentials_json=$(create_credentials_json)

if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "Successfully prepared credentials payload"
    
    # Send health report with credentials
    send_health_report "$credentials_json"
else
    echo "Failed to prepare credentials payload"
    exit 1
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Health check script for services running on ports 3000 and 8080

# Function to check if a port is responding
check_port() {
    local port=$1
    local status="Down"
    
    # Check if port is responding by attempting to connect with timeout
    if echo "" | nc -z -w 2 localhost $port; then
        status="Up"
    fi
    
    echo "Service on port $port: $status"
    # Return status for JSON (1 = Up, 0 = Down)
    if [ "$status" = "Up" ]; then
        return 1
    else
        return 0
    fi
}

# Function to source environment variables from a file
load_env() {
    local env_file=$1
    declare -gA env_vars
    
    if [[ -f "$env_file" ]]; then
        while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
            # Skip empty lines and comments
            if [[ -n "$key" && "$key" != \#* ]]; then
                env_vars["$key"]="$value"
            fi
        done < "$env_file"
    else
        echo "Warning: $env_file not found"
    fi
}

# Function to create JSON from the health check results
create_json_payload() {
    local port3000_status=$(check_port 3000; echo $?)
    local port8080_status=$(check_port 8080; echo $?)
    
    # Build JSON payload
    local json="{\"timestamp\":\"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\",\"services\":[{\"port\":3000,\"status\":$port3000_status},{\"port\":8080,\"status\":$port8080_status}],\"env\":{"
    
    # Add environment variables to JSON
    local first=true
    for key in "${!env_vars[@]}"; do
        if [ "$first" = true ]; then
            first=false
        else
            json+="","n        fi
        json+="\"$key\":\"${env_vars[$key]}\""
    done
    
    json+="}}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Main execution
echo "Health Check Report - $(date)"
echo "===================================="

# Check service on port 3000
check_port 3000

# Check service on port 8080
check_port 8080

# Load environment variables
load_env ".env.test"

# Create JSON payload and send to reporting endpoint
json_payload=$(create_json_payload)

echo "\nSending health report to http://localhost:3003/api/health-report"
curl -s -X POST \n    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \n    -d "$json_payload" \n    http://localhost:3003/api/health-report

    if curl -s --connect-timeout 5 http://localhost:$port >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Service on port $port is UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "ERROR: Service on port $port is DOWN"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "Error: .env.test file not found!"

PAYLOAD="{"
FIRST=true

while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
    # Skip empty lines and comments
    if [[ -z "$key" || "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    # Add comma if not the first item
    if [ "$FIRST" = true ]; then
        FIRST=false
    else
        PAYLOAD+=" , "
    fi
    
    # Append key-value pair to JSON

    echo "Successfully reported health and credentials (HTTP $HTTP_STATUS)"

# Health check script to verify if services on ports 3000 and 8080 are responding
# and report API credentials status to monitoring dashboard

check_port() {
    local port=$1
    echo "Checking service on port $port..."
    
    # Try to connect to the port using timeout and nc (netcat)
    if echo -n "" | timeout 5 nc -z localhost $port; then
        echo "OK: Service on port $port is UP"
        return 0
    else
        echo "ERROR: No response on port $port"
        return 1
    fi
}

# Function to read .env file and create JSON payload
create_credentials_payload() {
    local env_file="$1"
    local json="{\"timestamp\":\"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)\",\"services\":{\"port_3000\":$(check_port_status 3000),\"port_8080\":$(check_port_status 8080)},\"credentials\":{"
    
    local first=true
    while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
        # Skip comments and empty lines
        if [[ ! "$key" =~ ^\s*# ]] && [ -n "$key" ] && [ -n "$value" ]; then
            if [ "$first" = true ]; then
                first=false
            else
                json+="\n            ,"
            fi
            # Mask sensitive parts of the values
            local masked_value=$(mask_value "$value")
            json+="\"$key\":\"$masked_value\""
        fi
    done < "$env_file"
    
    json+="}}"
    echo "$json"
}

# Helper to check port status (returns true/false)
check_port_status() {
    local port=$1
    if echo -n "" | timeout 5 nc -z localhost $port; then
        echo "true"
    else
        echo "false"
    fi
}

# Function to mask sensitive values (show first 6 and last 6 chars, mask middle)
mask_value() {
    local value="$1"
    local length=${#value}
    if [ $length -le 12 ]; then
        echo "$value"
    else
        local prefix=${value:0:6}
        local suffix=${value: -6}
        echo "${prefix}***MASKED***${suffix}"
    fi
}

# Check service on port 3000
check_port 3000

# Check service on port 8080
check_port 8080

# Create JSON payload with credentials from .env.test
PAYLOAD=$(create_credentials_payload ".env.test")

# Send POST request to monitoring dashboard

     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d "$PAYLOAD"

exit 0

}
